nodes.name: Weekly Sunday 7AM Trigger
nodes.notes: Fires 07:00 every Sunday. Set workflow-level Settings > Timezone to Asia/Karachi (same as WF1) or this fires against server-local time instead.

nodes.name: Compute Week Window
nodes.notes: Aggregation window is the 7 days ending yesterday (Sat), same 'yesterday-anchored' convention WF1 uses for its daily window.

nodes.name: Read Trip_Data
nodes.notes: alwaysOutputData=true carried over from WF1's fix — an empty Trip_Data sheet must not silently kill every downstream node.

nodes.name: Aggregate Driver Weekly Stats
nodes.notes: Single output item bundling all drivers + top5 + fleet total, so downstream nodes can reference either the full list or the summary without a fragile merge.

nodes.name: Split Driver Rows
nodes.notes: Fans the single aggregation item out into one item per driver so the Sheets append node writes one row per driver.

nodes.name: Append Driver_Summary Rows
nodes.notes: Writes exactly the 12 confirmed live columns from the handoff doc's Driver_Summary schema. Driver_Full_Name intentionally excluded per that sheet's own note (join from Master_Drivers if ever needed).

nodes.name: Post Top5 Report to Slack
nodes.notes: Calls Slack's chat.postMessage directly via HTTP, same Bearer pattern as WF1's Traccar call -- avoids Slack-node credential-type/version mismatches. Channel is hardcoded to 'new-channel'; change the jsonBody if you want a different channel or to reference it dynamically.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ channel: "new-channel", text: `No Trip_Data rows found for the week ending ${$json.weekEnd}. Nothing to report or write to Driver_Summary this run.` }) }}

nodes.name: Post No-Data Notice to Slack
nodes.notes: Fires only on the False branch of 'Any Trips This Week?' -- so a quiet week is visible in Slack instead of silently writing nothing.
